codeReview.summary: ComparePage implementation is solid and meets all feature requirements. The component renders two audits side-by-side at /compare?a={id1}&b={id2}, shows trust score deltas with color coding, risk level comparison, finding count differences per scenario, and key metric comparisons. Route properly added in App.tsx. CSS follows the glass-morphism theme with responsive breakpoints. 10 comprehensive tests cover loading, error handling, data display, and metric calculations. A few non-blocking code quality issues noted below.

issues.description: Significant JSX duplication between Audit A column (lines 215-290) and Audit B column (lines 293-368). Nearly 75 lines of identical JSX structure repeated with only variable name changes (auditA→auditB). Should be refactored into an AuditColumn sub-component accepting audit data and column label as props.

Hero copy assumes both audits target the same URL: 'Side-by-side comparison of trust scores... across two audits of {auditA.audit.target_url}'. The 'Audit Details' section at line 448 also states 'Both audits target the same URL'. No validation enforces this constraint — if users compare audits of different URLs, the display would be misleading. Should either validate URLs match or display both URLs.

Hardcoded color values in .compare-column-title (#dce8f7), .compare-score-value (#dce8f7), and .scenario-compare-title (rgba(220,232,247,0.9)) instead of using existing CSS variables like var(--fg) or var(--fg-muted). Other pages use CSS variables consistently; these should too for maintainability.
No guard against comparing an audit with itself (a === b). If both query params are the same ID, the page renders the same audit twice without warning. Minor UX edge case.

Type assertions 'auditIdA as string' and 'auditIdB as string' at lines 77-78 are used inside loadAudits() even though the null check is in the parent scope. While safe due to the early return, a cleaner approach would be to capture the validated strings in const variables before the async function.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Commit 0b9110d includes duplicate files in node_modules/ethical-site-inspector-frontend/ (ComparePage.tsx, ComparePage.test.tsx, index.css, App.tsx all duplicated). This indicates a monorepo or symlink setup where frontend source is mirrored into node_modules, but this is not documented in AGENTS.md. Workers don't know about this and it creates confusing commit diffs (10 files changed instead of 4).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show 0b9110d --stat shows: frontend/src/pages/ComparePage.tsx (509 lines) AND node_modules/ethical-site-inspector-frontend/src/pages/ComparePage.tsx (509 lines) — identical files committed in both locations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker did NOT perform Step 5 (Manual Verification) of the frontend-dev skill procedure — interactiveChecks is empty in the handoff. However, handoff reports skillFeedback.followedProcedure as true with no deviations. The skill says to 'Start the dev server... Open the page in browser (via agent-browser or describe what you'd check)' but this was skipped entirely.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff JSON: 'interactiveChecks': []. Skill SKILL.md Step 5: 'Start the dev server: cd frontend && npm run dev -- --port 5173. Open the page in browser (via agent-browser or describe what you'd check).'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker transcript shows excessive repeated tool calls — the same files (Layout.tsx, FindingCard.tsx, format.ts) were read 10+ times each, and baseline tests were run 6+ times before the worker started implementing. This suggests the worker was stuck in a loop, possibly due to context window issues or tool result handling. The transcript also shows garbled output characters, indicating corruption.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows repeated patterns: 'Tool: Read file_path: Layout.tsx' appears ~12 times, 'Tool: Execute npm test' appears ~8 times before any code was written. Some assistant outputs contain garbled characters like '�^`�o<�^'

summary: ComparePage passes review. The implementation meets all feature requirements: route at /compare?a={id1}&b={id2}, two-column audit comparison, trust score delta with color coding, risk level comparison with severity pills, scenario breakdown with finding counts and deltas, key differences summary section, links to individual reports, and glass-morphism styling with responsive CSS. 10 tests provide good coverage of loading, error, and data states. Non-blocking issues include JSX duplication between the two audit columns (should extract a sub-component), hardcoded CSS colors instead of CSS variables, and an assumption that both audits target the same URL. The commit also includes duplicate files in node_modules/ which is a repo configuration issue. Worker skipped manual verification step from the skill procedure.
